content.full_text:
LATHAM & WATKINS
ATTORNEYS AT LAW

555 SOUTH FLOWER STREET
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90071

PAUL F. WATKINS (1893-1970)
DANA LATHAM (1898-1974)

TELEPHONE (213) 485-1234

ORANGE COUNTY OFFICE

CABLE ADDRESS: LATHWAT
TWX 910-321-3733

660 NEWPORT CENTER DRIVE

FOX (213) 689-2098

TELEPHONE (714) 752-9100

NEWPORT BEACH, CALIFORNIA 92660

October 1, 1976

Kenneth Finney, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
1100 K Street Building
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Ken:

This is to confirm our conversation of today
concerning our client which has recently discovered that
it failed to report certain cash contributions to a
political committee in 1975.

You have informed me as follows: Our client,
or I, acting on its behalf, should write the Commission
and explain what contributions our client inadvertently
failed to report. You will respond with a letter directing
our client to file the 1975 data along with the timely filing
of its 1976 contribution report. Your present expectation
is that in the letter you will state that our client is not
subject to sanctions under the Political Reform Act of 1974,
as amended, over which the Commission has jurisdiction.
Finally, you suggest that we file with the Secretary of
State not only our reports but also copies of our correspondence with you. It is also your present understanding
that the Secretary of State will impose no sanctions on
our client for the inadvertent failure to report.

If this letter misstates our understanding, please
let me know as soon as possible.

Very truly yours,

Reed E. Hundt
of LATHAM & WATKINS

sections.question_synthetic: An attorney seeks confirmation that their client, which inadvertently failed to report certain cash contributions to a political committee in 1975, will not be subject to sanctions under the Political Reform Act of 1974 if they file the unreported data with their 1976 contribution report.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/correspondence letter from an attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC advice response. The letter confirms an oral conversation with Kenneth Finney regarding compliance procedures for unreported contributions. No formal question or conclusion sections are present; the document is structured as a confirmation letter summarizing FPPC's informal guidance.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: An attorney seeks confirmation that their client, which inadvertently failed to report certain cash contributions to a political committee in 1975, will not be subject to sanctions under the Political Reform Act of 1974 if they file the unreported data with their 1976 contribution report.

embedding.summary: An attorney from Latham & Watkins writes to FPPC staff to confirm an informal conversation regarding their client's inadvertent failure to report 1975 political contributions, seeking confirmation that no sanctions will apply if the client files the missing data with its 1976 report.
